ApiVersionRange.description: The inclusive gateway API version range this generation serves.

minimum.description: Lowest served gateway API version from the supported enum.

maximum.description: Highest served gateway API version from the supported enum.

ApiVersionRange.title: ApiVersionRange

ComponentAsset.description: One immutable source artifact used to assemble the base closure.

license.description: SPDX-style license identifier for the asset.
license.title: License

digest.description: Digest of the immutable source artifact bytes used for assembly (runtime archive, A2A wheel, or ACP package); installed-tree integrity is governed by later release/SBOM contracts.
digest.title: Digest

ComponentAsset.title: ComponentAsset

ComponentAssetKind.description:
The base-closure asset kinds a complete capsule declares.

A capsule ships a bundled CPython runtime, the locked A2A distribution, a
bundled Node.js runtime, and the pinned ACP adapter. The pinned versions are
:data:`CPYTHON_VERSION_PIN`, :data:`NODEJS_VERSION_PIN`, and
:data:`ACP_VERSION_PIN`.
ComponentAssetKind.title: ComponentAssetKind

ComponentCompatibility.description: Protocol and schema compatibility facts a consumer must honour.

api_versions.description: Gateway API version range served by this generation.

migration_range.description: Alembic base/head revision range packaged in this generation.

ComponentCompatibility.title: ComponentCompatibility

ComponentEntrypoints.description: The dashboard-owned gateway launch and the caller-owned MCP launch.
ComponentEntrypoints.title: ComponentEntrypoints

ComponentIdentity.description: The component's distribution name and its own version.
ComponentIdentity.title: ComponentIdentity

ConsistencyGroup.description:
The mutable stores the updater snapshots and restores as one group.

The declaration binds membership, per-store derivability, and each store's
schema authority into the manifest so a consumer knows exactly which mutable
stores move together across a generation change, and which (if any) may be
omitted because they are provably derivable. The current generation declares
both stores non-derivable, so both are mandatory members.

stores.description: The declared consistency-group members, one per store kind.
stores.title: Stores

ConsistencyGroup.title: ConsistencyGroup

DependencyLockIdentity.description: Digests pinning the resolved Python and JavaScript dependency graphs.
DependencyLockIdentity.title: DependencyLockIdentity

DigestAlgorithm.description: The digest algorithm governing every hash in a manifest.
DigestAlgorithm.title: DigestAlgorithm

GatewayApiVersion.description: Gateway API versions implemented by the production route surface.
GatewayApiVersion.title: GatewayApiVersion

console_script.description: Console-script name declared by the distribution.
console_script.title: Console Script

relative_command.description: Bounded argv path segments relative to the capsule root.

items.description: One portable, non-rooted capsule path segment; Windows device basenames and platform-invalid characters are forbidden.

relative_command.title: Relative Command

MigrationRange.description: The Alembic revision range the generation's package migrations cover.
MigrationRange.title: MigrationRange

MutableStore.description: One declared mutable, schema-bearing consistency-group store.

kind.description: Which schema-bearing mutable store this declares.

derivable.description: Current stores are mandatory and cannot be omitted. A future derivable form requires a separate tagged evidence contract.
derivable.title: Derivable

schema_authority.description: Authority governing this store's schema revision. The Alembic authority reconciles with compatibility.migration_range rather than duplicating the base and head.

schema_version.description: Exact schema revision required by this generation: the packaged Alembic head or the project-owned checkpoint semantic version.
schema_version.title: Schema Version

MutableStore.title: MutableStore

MutableStoreKind.description:
The schema-bearing mutable stores forming the desktop consistency group.

These are the stores the dashboard's external updater snapshots and restores
as one receipt-verifiable group across a generation change.
``PRIMARY_DATABASE`` is the Alembic-versioned application database;
``CHECKPOINT_DATABASE`` is the LangGraph checkpoint database carrying the
state-driven-development state.
MutableStoreKind.title: MutableStoreKind

StandaloneMcpEntrypoint.description: The caller-owned standalone MCP launch surface.

console_script.description: Console-script name declared by the distribution.
console_script.title: Console Script

relative_command.description: Bounded argv path segments relative to the capsule root.

items.description: One portable, non-rooted capsule path segment; Windows device basenames and platform-invalid characters are forbidden.

relative_command.title: Relative Command

StandaloneMcpEntrypoint.title: StandaloneMcpEntrypoint

StoreSchemaAuthority.description:
What governs a mutable store's schema revision.

``ALEMBIC_MIGRATION_RANGE`` reconciles with the manifest's
:attr:`ComponentCompatibility.migration_range`, which is the single authority
for the primary database's schema-revision facts; a store bearing this
authority does not restate the base and head. ``CHECKPOINTER_SCHEMA`` denotes
the LangGraph checkpointer's own schema, which the migration range does not
cover.
StoreSchemaAuthority.title: StoreSchemaAuthority

TargetTriple.description:
The five accepted desktop capsule targets, as Rust-style triples.

The triples match the platform vocabulary already used by the locked
dependency closure (see ``desktop_tests/test_dependency_closure.py``).
TargetTriple.title: TargetTriple

description:
The versioned desktop component manifest consumed by dashboard packaging.

The manifest is the entire boundary the dashboard reads about an A2A
generation. It is deterministic and self-describing: given the same inputs
the emitter produces a byte-identical document, so a release-set receipt can
pin a generation by manifest digest.

contract_version.description: Canonical MAJOR.MINOR grammar version; consumers require the same major and a declared minor no newer than they support.
contract_version.title: Contract Version

compatibility.description: Served API range and packaged Alembic migration range.

consistency_group.description: The mutable, schema-bearing stores the external updater snapshots and restores as one group; the primary store's schema authority reconciles with compatibility.migration_range.

entrypoints.description: Gateway and standalone MCP launch surfaces.

digest_algorithm.description: Algorithm governing every digest in this manifest.

assets.description: The exact four-kind base closure with pinned runtime versions, licenses, and digests.
assets.title: Assets

dependency_lock.description: Digests pinning the Python and JavaScript dependency graphs.

title: ComponentManifest
x-vaultspec-invariants: identity.version must equal the a2a-distribution asset version; enforced by the production model because JSON Schema Draft 2020-12 has no standard cross-field equality keyword
the primary-database schema_version must equal compatibility.migration_range.head; enforced by the production model because JSON Schema Draft 2020-12 has no standard cross-field equality keyword
